Financial Performance

Step-by-step savings calculation from historical benchmark to earned savings/losses.

Step Description Value MSSP Median Peer Median
1 Assigned Beneficiaries 41,830 p88 13,159 19,799
2 Historical Benchmark (per capita) $9,925 p11 $11,439 $11,243
3 Regional Adjustment +$209
4 Prior Savings Adjustment +-
5 Adjustment Category Regional Adjustment
6 Updated Benchmark (per capita) $11,626 p13 $13,279 $14,413
7 Total Benchmark (all beneficiaries) $477,097,671
8 Total Expenditure (all beneficiaries) $433,433,268
9 Per Capita Expenditure (PY) $10,562 p7 $12,693 $13,231
10 Generated Savings / Losses $43,664,403 $6,931,306 —
11 Savings Rate 9.15% p85 4.21% 3.93%
12 Minimum Savings Rate (threshold) 2.28%
13 Guardrail Applied —
14 Final Shared Savings Rate 40.00%
15 Final Shared Loss Rate -
16 Earned Savings / Losses $17,116,446 $3,685,876 —
Revenue-to-Expense Category Low Revenue

Annual per-beneficiary expenditure across benchmark years (BY1–BY3) and the performance year (PY). Includes Parts A and B FFS spending.

Population Segment BY1BY2BY3PY Trend Risk-Adj. Expected Difference

Aged, Non-Dual $8,478 $8,747 $8,862 $10,320 ↑ $11,830 −$1,510
Total $10,562 $12,693 $13,231

Risk Scores

Hierarchical Condition Category risk scores. A score of 1.0 = national average. Below 1.0 means the population is healthier than average.

CMS-HCC Risk Scores

Population Segment BY1BY2BY3PY Trend Coding Gap MSSP Med.
End-Stage Renal Disease (ESRD) 0.994 1.018 1.018 1.008 → +0.024 0.967
Disabled 1.019 0.966 0.969 0.913 ↓ -0.069 0.934
Aged, Dual-Eligible 1.138 1.093 1.076 0.987 ↓ -0.033 0.954
Aged, Non-Dual 1.011 0.985 0.976 0.981 → +0.020 0.983

Demographic Risk Scores

Demographic-only risk scores (age/sex/Medicaid status). Compared with HCC risk scores, the difference reflects clinical coding intensity.

Population Segment BY3PY
End-Stage Renal Disease (ESRD) — 0.984
Disabled — 0.982
Aged, Dual-Eligible — 1.020
Aged, Non-Dual — 0.961

Risk Redistribution Weights (PY)

Fraction of the ACO's total risk attributable to each population segment.

ESRD Weight 2.2%
Disabled Weight 4.2%
Aged Dual Weight 3.0%
Aged Non-Dual Weight 90.5%

Clinical Quality Measures

MSSP quality measures. Measures marked "lower is better" are inverted — a lower score indicates better performance.

Measure This ACO MSSP Median Peer Median
Overall Quality Score (Composite) p45 82.31 83.13 82.25
All-Cause Unplanned Admissions (risk-adjusted rate) (lower is better) ? p72 0.16 0.15 0.15

Peer Cohort

30 similar mid-size, BASIC-track, average acuity, physician-led ACOs

View 30 peer ACOs
A5444A3864A5062A3759A3873A1247A4947A2695A3623A2311A3378A3250A4943A5013A5375A2620A4824A3588A5058A1490A3723A3797A3547A2706A5329A5178A4620A4888A1652A2645

Peers selected using weighted distance across 15 structural features (population acuity, composition, scale, care infrastructure, assignment type) within the BASIC track partition. Learn more

Methodology

Data source: MSSP ACO Performance PY2024 (dataset ID: mssp-performance). Vintage: PY2024. Downloaded: 2026-04-13. Rows in source: 476.

MSSP ACO Performance reports financial and quality performance for Accountable Care Organizations participating in the Medicare Shared Savings Program. Savings/losses are calculated against risk-adjusted, regionally-weighted benchmarks.

Full methodology →

MIT License. Free. No ads. No login.
